challenges.title: Hello World
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that prints "Hello, World!" to the console. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Include the iostream header
- Use cout to print the message
- Have a main() function that returns 0
challenges.test_code:
assert '#include <iostream>' in cpp, 'Should include the iostream header'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should use cout to produce output'
assert 'Hello' in cpp, 'Should print a Hello message'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Use #include <iostream> at the top of your program
cout << "Hello, World!" sends text to the console

challenges.title: Personal Info Output
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that outputs your name on one line and your age on the next line. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Include the iostream header
- Use cout with endl (or '\n') to print on separate lines
- Output at least two separate cout statements or use endl between them
challenges.test_code:
assert '#include <iostream>' in cpp, 'Should include the iostream header'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should use cout for output'
assert cpp.count('cout') >= 2 or 'endl' in cpp or '\\n' in cpp, 'Should output on multiple lines using endl or multiple cout statements'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Use endl after your first cout to move to a new line: cout << "Name" << endl;
You can chain multiple items with <<, for example: cout << "Age: " << 25 << endl;
